title: ee.graph.rule_provenance_ego.v1
description: Bipartite ego subgraph for a single procedural rule. Produced by `ee::graph::bipartite_provenance::compute_rule_provenance_ego`. The bare payload returned by the helper; the planned `ee rule provenance <rule-id>` CLI surface (tracked by bd-3eymh) will wrap this inside the standard `ee.response.v2` envelope. Collections are deterministically sorted (`citedMemories` by `memoryId`, `coCitingRules` by `ruleId`, `sharedMemoryIds` by memory id) so the JSON serialization is byte-stable across runs against the same projection (J7 contract).

schema.description: Schema version pin. Bumps when this document's shape changes.

ruleId.description: Center rule the ego subgraph is rooted at. Echoed back from the caller-supplied identifier even when the rule is not present in the bipartite projection.

citedMemories.description: Memories the center rule cites (the 1-hop ring on the bipartite projection). Sorted by `memoryId` ascending. Empty when `status` is not `available`.

coCitingRules.description: Peer rules that share at least one memory with the center rule (the 2-hop ring). Sorted by `ruleId` ascending. The center rule itself is never included. Empty when `status` is not `available`.

egoStatus.description: Honest enumeration of why the ego subgraph might be empty. `available` means the rule node was found and traversed (even if it cited nothing). `rule_not_found` separates 'this rule has no provenance edges yet' from 'this rule isn't in the bipartite projection at all', and `not_a_rule_node` fires when the caller passed a memory id where a rule id was expected. Agents use the distinction to decide whether to retry after a steward refresh or to surface a structured error.

memoryId.description: Memory cited by the center rule.

otherRuleCount.description: Count of OTHER rules in the bipartite projection that also cite this memory (excluding the center rule). `0` means the memory is anchored solely by the center.

ruleId.description: Peer rule id. Always different from the center rule's id.

sharedMemoryCount.description: Length of `sharedMemoryIds`. A peer that shares zero memories with the center is never included in `coCitingRules`, so this is always at least 1 when the entry appears.

sharedMemoryIds.description: Deterministic intersection of this peer's cited memories with the center's cited memories. Sorted by memory id ascending, deduplicated.
